purpose: Manages PTY-backed terminal sessions for the in-app Terminal tab and worker terminals. Spawns a real shell (zsh/bash) via node-pty with the given cwd/cols/rows/shell, streams output to the renderer over the `terminal-output` channel, accepts user input via writeTerminal(id, data), supports resize and kill. Each session is keyed by a generated `term_<timestamp>` ID and tracked in activeTerminals.
gotchas: ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE is deleted from the env — without this the spawned shell would inherit Electron-as-Node mode and the user's interactive shell config (.zshrc) might misbehave.
Default shell is `process.env.SHELL || '/bin/zsh'` — on Linux servers without zsh installed the default fallback fails to spawn. Pass opts.shell explicitly in that case.
Two terminals spawned in the same millisecond would have id collision (both 'term_<sameMs>'). Practical risk is near-zero but theoretically possible during rapid programmatic spawn.

`SYNAPSE_DASHBOARD_ID` is injected into env when opts.dashboardId is supplied — same as ClaudeCodeService — so PreToolUse hooks can enforce dashboard isolation if the user runs `claude` inside the terminal.
node-pty is a NATIVE module — it must be rebuilt for each Electron version (electron-rebuild). A version mismatch crashes at require('node-pty').

There is no scrollback buffer — every onData chunk is broadcast immediately. Renderer-side xterm.js is the source of truth for history; if the renderer disconnects (window reload), the terminal output between disconnect and reconnect is LOST.
patterns: PTY-based terminal sessions with streaming via the `terminal-output` push channel.
Two-channel push protocol: `terminal-output` (per-chunk data) and `terminal-exit` (one-shot at process exit).
ID-keyed activeTerminals map with cleanup-on-exit — onExit handler deletes the entry before broadcasting.
killTerminal calls proc.kill() with no signal arg — node-pty defaults to SIGHUP which closes the PTY cleanly.
conventions: Default cols=80, rows=24 — standard terminal geometry.
All public fns log lifecycle transitions with `[TerminalService]` prefix.
spawnTerminal returns minimal info, full state lives in activeTerminals (fetched via getActiveTerminals).
